response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software engineer with web, mobile, and some embedded experience, but the role requires deep hardware/SoC expertise — performance architecture for AI accelerators, SoC/IP modeling, DNN training/inference optimization, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no evidence of SoC modeling, AI accelerator work, SIMD/MIMD architecture, GPU internals, or deep-learning workload performance analysis, and the educational background (BS in CS) without the heavy hardware/architecture specialization makes this a clear mismatch for a Principal Performance Architect role.
strengths: Strong full-stack software engineering background across multiple languages and stacks
Some low-level/systems exposure via DLang microservices and C/C++ at National Instruments
Solid engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testing, and craftsmanship focus
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or chip architecture experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or DNN training/inference optimization background
Profile is application/web software, not hardware performance architecture at Principal level

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DNN workload optimization background
